summary: Iteration 30 net-sales display fix verified. Backend: /api/kpis still exposes total_sales/net_sales/gross_sales; /api/analytics/sell-through-by-location now returns total_sales == net_sales per row; /api/search/ask intent=stores uses net_sales via _fetch_stores. Frontend: Overview & Locations KPI Total Sales headlines MATCH (KES 3,348,314) confirming the cross-page consistency goal. Locations KPI card shows the required 'Net of returns & discounts' subtitle. Data Quality flag row shows net sales (KES 208,997) alongside KES 73,050 returns for Online - Shop Zetu, with return-rate denominator preserved. All 6 pytest cases passed.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:1934-1956 — /api/analytics/sell-through-by-location correctly sets total_sales = net_sales (falls back to gross when net missing). Clear intent-preserving comment. OK.
useKpis.js fetchKpis normaliser swaps total_sales -> net_sales and preserves gross_sales under gross_sales. Cache keys unchanged, so previously cached gross values aren't served stale. OK.
lib/api.js normaliseSalesRows preserves r.total_sales under gross_sales before overwriting — correct. Note: helper falls back to total_sales if net_sales is null/missing (safe default).

ask.py:384 _fetch_stores hardcodes a 28-day window and ignores filters.date_from/date_to — not a regression of this fix, but worth noting the LLM cannot actually scope by user's date filter for the 'stores' intent.
Locations.jsx:150,164 prefers rawKpis.net_sales directly (in addition to the normaliser at fetch) — defensive duplication, fine.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 30 fixed the display-level gross/net sales swap across channel & location views. Spot-verified facts: (a) /api/kpis returns { total_sales=97.84M, net_sales=90.11M, gross_sales=93.25M } for wide windows; (b) /api/analytics/sell-through-by-location rows have total_sales == net_sales; (c) frontend Overview & Locations KPI 'Total Sales' headlines are identical for the same filter window; (d) Locations KPI subtitle 'Net of returns & discounts' rendered correctly; (e) DataQuality flag row preserves gross in the return-rate denominator while showing net in the displayed sales column. Style/customer lifetime total_sales NOT touched (and not in scope).
rca of the issue: Upstream /kpis.total_sales and /sales-summary rows expose total_sales as 'gross minus discounts' which still includes returns; for channel/location headline 'Total Sales' the correct value is net_sales. Fix implemented at two layers: (1) backend sell-through-by-location endpoint now materialises total_sales = net_sales so direct consumers see net by default; (2) frontend normalisers (useKpis.fetchKpis + lib/api.normaliseSalesRows) swap total_sales with net_sales at fetch time, preserving original gross as gross_sales for return-rate math. This guarantees every page reading these shared helpers displays the same net figure.
